response.justification: The candidate is a versatile generalist with strong full-stack web experience (TypeScript/Node.js) and some C/C++ and embedded exposure (LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms, a C++ wave-field-synthesis system). However, the role's hard requirements are largely unmet: there is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency, no security-domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption, tamper detection), no LAN-switch or L2/L3 networking work, and no Linux kernel source-code modification. C is a minor, dated part of their background rather than a core strength for UNIX/embedded development. The mismatch on must-have qualifications makes this a poor fit for a mid-senior embedded security role.
strengths: Broad multi-domain engineering experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded/hardware work
Demonstrated C/C++ experience (LabWindows/CVI IDE components, C++ audio system) and Linux familiarity
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and active knowledge-sharing
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency, a hard requirement (only English/Romanian fluent, basic German)
No security-feature experience (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, tamper/attack detection) or LAN-switch/L2-L3 networking
No Linux kernel investigation or source-code modification experience; C is peripheral, not the primary skill
